fact_verdicts.fact: Action id is gov_action1k02990lhw6wh74t7c6ufw3mqaek9ujtvyan99dj5qv5kvcs7pn8s5z9qdza; action type is TreasuryWithdrawals; proposed epoch is 638; expires after epoch 645; treasury amount is 540750000000 lovelace.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 0.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor title is "Withdraw 540,750 ada for UTxO RPC by TxPipe: Maintaining Cardano’s Integratio..."
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor document title.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the withdrawal funds "UTxO RPC by TxPipe: Maintaining Cardano’s Integration Standard, Year 2" and was submitted by Intersect on behalf of the vendor.
fact_verdicts.note: Both statements appear in the anchor abstract.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor motivation requests "420,000 ADA (plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve)" to fund "a part-time maintainer for UTxO RPC" over 12 months at an annual rate of "$105,000 USD".
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor motivation.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states UTxO RPC is an open interface specification for UTxO-based blockchains using Protocol Buffers and SDKs in Rust, Go, Node.js, .NET, Haskell, and Python.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor motivation and rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states UTxO RPC has been adopted by Amaru, Dingo, and the Haskell Cardano node.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor motivation and rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the project maintains "10+ repositories" covering the core specification, SDKs, documentation, and provider integrations, and is open-source under Apache 2.0.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor motivation.

fact_verdicts.fact: The stated maintenance scope covers protobuf specification maintenance, official SDK maintenance, community support, and AI-friendly documentation and integration resources.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the four maintenance areas in the anchor motivation.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Intersect Budget Process section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor states the requested amount does not, at time of submission, alone or in aggregate, breach the applicable "350M Net Change Limit" covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Net Change Limit Compliance section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The budget table lists WP1 - UTxO RPC Maintenance and Enhancement at 525,000 ADA, Intersect Budget Administration fee at 15,750 ADA, and Total at 540,750 ADA.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Budget Summary table.

fact_verdicts.fact: The on-chain gov_action rewards field sends 540750000000 lovelace to stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the anchor onChain gov_action rewards field.

fact_verdicts.fact: The on-chain context says stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v appears in 14 withdrawal proposals totalling 57273260000000 lovelace, first 2026-06-23 16:14:33 and last 2026-06-26 13:51:52.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 recipient history.

fact_verdicts.fact: The on-chain context reports DRep explicit ballot counts so far as yes=43, no=4, abstain=1, not stake-weighted.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 1 reports yes=46, no=4, abstain=1, but the source note says differing ballot counts are moving snapshots and must be treated as not_found, not contradicted.

fact_verdicts.fact: The on-chain context reports 36-epoch treasury inflow of 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals of 361435336000000 lovelace, and ratio 2.275492.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 treasury flow.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says audit and oversight costs are included within overhead and that the Intersect administration fee covers administrative oversight.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Audit & Oversight section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says independent oversight will be provided through Intersect and technically capable third parties, including reporting obligations and milestone-based disbursement controls.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Audit & Oversight section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says TxPipe has received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals across Funds 9 through 14, with 26 successfully delivered and 4 currently under development and on schedule.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says TxPipe received Intersect 2025 open-source maintenance funding for Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC, all being delivered on schedule.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor names a 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address, payment address, and Project Specific Smart Contract payment address.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor lists all three addresses under Intersect Budget Management Tooling.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says the Oversight Committee consists of Sundae Labs, Cardano Foundation, Dquadrant, NMKR, Sundial, and Eternl.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Specifics section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor describes high-level TRSC/PSSC authorization thresholds for Fund and PSSC Modify, TRSC Disburse, TRSC Pause and Resume, TRSC Sweep, and TRSC Reorganize.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor lists high-level permissions for each named action.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says, after enactment, funding will be directed into the TRSC stake address, later transferred to the PSSC when the legal contract is prepared and the vendor is ready, and that all milestones will be outlined within the metadata.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Processes section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The anchor says a dashboard is available at treasury.sundae.fi for community auditing and tracking metrics related to the withdrawn ada.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the final dashboard statement in the anchor.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine says treasury stewardship and downside protection ranks above evidence quality and public-benefit ecosystem growth in the values hierarchy.
fact_verdicts.note: The values hierarchy ranks treasury stewardship second, evidence quality third, and ecosystem growth fourth.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine rejects first-come-first-served budget models and adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the doctrine Core Position.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine says, under the current soft gate, a TreasuryWithdrawals action without a complete dossier is scored directionally with an explicit -0.10 caution penalty, and incomplete diligence is recorded as uncertainty.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Dossier Gate Posture soft-gate text.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine states that if work can be substantially accelerated by AI tooling, the budget should reflect post-AI costs, not legacy labor models.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Foundational Axiom section.

fact_verdicts.fact: The doctrine says proposals should be funded by DRep approval weight descending within the available budget, not by submission order.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Prioritization section.

verification.summary: All checked facts are supported except fact 14, which cites a moving ballot-count snapshot that differs from the supplied on-chain context. Per the source instruction, that is not_found rather than contradicted and is not a material discrepancy.
